mission.title: Install and Configure KubeAI
mission.description: KubeAI is an AI Inference Operator for Kubernetes designed to serve machine learning models, including LLMs and embeddings, in production environments. It simplifies the deployment and scaling of ML models while optimizing performance through intelligent load balancing. Key trade-offs include the need for sufficient hardware resources and the potential complexity of managing model configurations.

steps.title: Add the KubeAI Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the KubeAI Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install KubeAI easily.

```bash

helm repo add kubeai https://charts.kubeai.org

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the KubeAI Helm repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install KubeAI CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for KubeAI to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/substratusai/kubeai/main/deploy/crds/kubeai_v1alpha1_kubeai_crd.yaml

```

> This command installs the necessary CRDs for KubeAI.

steps.title: Install KubeAI using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs KubeAI in the specified namespace, allowing you to manage AI inference workloads.

```bash

helm install kubeai kubeai/kubeai --namespace kubeai --create-namespace --version 0.23.1

```

> Installs KubeAI in the 'kubeai' namespace.

steps.title: Create an example Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates an example Custom Resource (CR) for KubeAI, which defines the AI model to be served.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kubeai.substratus.ai/v1alpha1
kind: KubeAI
metadata:
  name: example-kubeai
spec:
  model: 'your-model-name'
  replicas: 2
EOF

```

> Replace 'your-model-name' with the actual model you want to serve.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kubeai.substratus.ai/v1alpha1
kind: KubeAI
metadata:
  name: example-kubeai
spec:
  model: 'your-model-name'
  replicas: 2
EOF

steps.title: Verify KubeAI installation
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the KubeAI deployment and ensures that the Custom Resource is ready.

```bash

kubectl get kubeai -n kubeai

kubectl get pods -n kubeai

```

> Verify that the KubeAI instance is running.
> Check that all KubeAI pods are in a running state.

uninstall.title: Uninstall KubeAI
uninstall.description:
This step removes KubeAI and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all KubeAI-related data and configurations.

```bash

helm uninstall kubeai --namespace kubeai

kubectl delete namespace kubeai

```

> Uninstalls KubeAI from the specified namespace.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade KubeAI
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your configurations. This step will upgrade KubeAI to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get kubeai -n kubeai -o yaml > kubeai-backup.yaml

helm upgrade kubeai kubeai/kubeai --namespace kubeai --version 0.23.1

kubectl get kubeai -n kubeai

```

> Backup the current KubeAI configuration.
> Upgrades KubeAI to the specified version.
> Verify that the KubeAI instance is still running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get kubeai -n kubeai -o yaml > kubeai-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'KubeAI instance not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The KubeAI instance may not have been created or may have been deleted.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the Custom Resource was created successfully and check the namespace.

troubleshooting.title: Pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The model specified in the Custom Resource may not be available or correctly configured.

**Fix:**
Check the model name and ensure it is accessible. Review pod logs for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to KubeAI service'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The KubeAI service may not be running or correctly exposed.

**Fix:**
Check the service status and ensure the correct ports are open.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources to schedule the KubeAI pods.

**Fix:**
Ensure that your cluster meets the minimum resource requirements for KubeAI.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new model types and improved load balancing algorithms.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the KubeAI instance listed with 'Running' status when executing 'kubectl get kubeai -n kubeai'.

EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster with sufficient resources and the required tools installed.
